name: validation-set-quality-eval-v1
description: EP004 quality eval. Fixed, VERIFIABLE agent-style tasks: every task has a deterministic grader (a number, a letter, a JSON shape, or code that must pass asserts) so 'quality' is MEASURED, not vibe-graded or judged by another LLM. Same tasks hit every model at temperature 0 so the score is reproducible. Spread of difficulty across math / reasoning / code / format-following / extraction so the set discriminates a 3B from an 8B from a 14B. Freeze this file once a video is recorded against it.
grading: Answer-extraction is deliberately lenient (we take the LAST number, the JSON object/array even if fenced, the final A-D letter) so we score REASONING, not format pedantry -- except the 'format' bucket, where producing clean JSON IS the skill. See run_quality.py for the exact graders.

tasks.prompt: A server processes 1,250 requests per minute. How many requests does it process in 8 hours? Reply with just the number.

A team has 3 engineers. Each writes 40 lines of code per hour and works 6 hours a day. Over 5 working days, how many total lines of code does the whole team write? Think it through, then give the final number on its own line.
An API costs $0.002 per 1,000 tokens. You process 3.5 million tokens. What is the total cost in dollars? Reply with only the number, no dollar sign.
A GPU generates 70 tokens per second. How many seconds does it take to generate 4,200 tokens? Reply with only the number.

Four people finished a race. Ana finished before Ben. Carl finished after Ben but before Dana. Who finished last?
(A) Ana  (B) Ben  (C) Carl  (D) Dana
Answer with a single letter.

Which value is the largest?
(A) 2^10  (B) 10^3  (C) 3^6  (D) 5^4
Answer with a single letter.

A process holds a 512 MB cache and you have 8 GB of RAM. Roughly how many such caches fit in RAM if nothing else used memory? (1 GB = 1024 MB.)
(A) 4  (B) 8  (C) 16  (D) 64
Answer with a single letter.
Write a Python function `solve(n)` that returns the sum of all integers from 1 to n inclusive (and 0 if n < 1). Reply with only the code.
Write a Python function `count_vowels(s)` that returns the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) in the string s. Reply with only the code.
Write a Python function `is_palindrome(s)` that returns True if s is a palindrome ignoring case and any non-alphanumeric characters, otherwise False. Reply with only the code.

cases: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama
Was it a car or a cat I saw?

tasks.prompt: Write a Python function `two_sum(nums, target)` that returns the indices [i, j] with i < j of the two numbers in the list nums that add up to target. Assume exactly one solution exists. Reply with only the code.

Extract the person's details from this sentence and return ONLY a JSON object with keys "name" (string) and "age" (integer). No prose, no code fence.
Sentence: Maria is a 34-year-old data scientist.

Classify the sentiment of the text. Return ONLY a JSON object {"sentiment": "positive" | "negative" | "neutral"}. No prose.
Text: The deployment failed twice and support never replied.
Return ONLY a JSON array of the prime numbers strictly between 10 and 20, in ascending order. No prose, no code fence.
Reply with ONLY the numeric HTTP status code that means "Service Unavailable".
